terms.definition: Neural architecture using self-attention to process all tokens in parallel. Foundation of BERT, Claude, and LLaMA.
Mechanism where each token computes relevance scores against every other token to build contextual representations.
Transformer half designed for understanding input bidirectionally. BERT uses encoder-only stacks.
Transformer half designed for autoregressive generation with causal masking. Used by Claude and LLaMA.
BERT's pretraining objective: predict randomly masked tokens using bidirectional context.
Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback — aligns models using human preference rankings and reward models.
Anthropic's method where models critique and revise outputs against written principles, reducing harmful responses.
Next-token prediction objective used by decoder models. Each token only attends to prior tokens.
Grouped-Query Attention — LLaMA's efficient attention variant with fewer key-value heads than query heads.
Massive Multitask Language Understanding — benchmark spanning 57 academic subjects for reasoning evaluation.
Code generation benchmark with 164 Python programming problems evaluated by unit tests.
Rotary Positional Embeddings — encodes token position via rotation in embedding space, enabling long contexts.
